mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side contraction and weakening corporate output, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading below 2.5% signals disinflationary progress that reduces equity discount rate uncertainty, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of low-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening labor demand and reduced household income, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive direction in > 55% of payroll decline months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of overlapping observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production alongside declining housing starts signals synchronized supply and demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of synchronized decline instances.
